title: Visualizing Open Sets in a Metric Space
description: A diagram showing how an open set contains a neighborhood around every one of its points, contrasting it with non-open sets that lack this property.
relationships: For every point x in the open set U, there exists an ε > 0 such that the entire neighborhood B(x, ε) is contained within U
A closed set F fails this property at its boundary points; for a point y on the boundary of F, no neighborhood B(y, ε) is entirely contained within F
